title: caltha palustris subsp. violacea
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. violacea, also known as marsh marigold or violet marsh marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Caltha palustris subsp. violacea include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and digestive problems.
